title: Fix drag-and-drop in save diagram so only the thumbnail preview image is draggable

requirements.summary: Fix drag-and-drop in AttackPathToolbar and related save diagram UI so that only the thumbnail preview image is draggable onto the canvas, not the surrounding container or UI elements
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging the thumbnail preview image of a diagram initiates a drag event and allows it to be dropped onto the canvas.
Dragging the label, border, background, or any non-thumbnail part of the diagram element does not initiate a drag.
The drag-and-drop canvas drop behavior works correctly when the thumbnail is dragged onto the AttackPathCanvas.
No regression in existing toolbar icon drag-and-drop functionality.

file_operations.description: Modify the saved diagram rendering logic to set draggable={false} on the parent container and all non-thumbnail elements (labels, borders, buttons). Apply draggable={true} and drag event handlers (onDragStart, onDragEnd) exclusively to the element that displays the diagram thumbnail preview. Ensure the drag data transfer includes the diagram state so it can be dropped onto the AttackPathCanvas.

If the sidebar also displays draggable diagram thumbnails, apply the same fix: set draggable={false} on parent containers and non-thumbnail elements, and draggable={true} with drag handlers only on the thumbnail element. Ensure consistency with the AttackPathToolbar drag-and-drop behavior.

If the load diagram dialog displays draggable thumbnails, apply the same pattern: set draggable={false} on the container and non-thumbnail UI elements, and draggable={true} with drag handlers exclusively on the thumbnail element to allow dragging the preview onto the canvas.

Review the onDrop handler to ensure it correctly receives and processes diagram state from dragged thumbnail images. Verify that the drop behavior works when only the element is draggable, and that no regression occurs for existing icon drag-and-drop functionality.
